update_summary: Second-pass novelty check. Cand_a revised proposal incorporates all April 10 recommendations — novelty upgraded to 7/10. Cand_b downgraded from 6/10 to 4/10 due to CD4LM (arXiv:2601.02236) and CDLM (arXiv:2511.19269) occupying the consistency distillation for LLaDA-8B space. Cand_c unchanged at 8/10.

candidates.title: ComposeAccel: Binary Composability, Super-Multiplicative Synergy, and Failure Modes of Training-Free MDM Acceleration
candidates.score_change_from_prior_report: +2 (from 5/10 to 7/10 due to successful proposal revision)

collisions.paper: Gao et al., 2025. 'Self Speculative Decoding for Diffusion Large Language Models (SSD).' arXiv:2510.04147. Published 2025-10-05.
collisions.overlap: Training-free, no-auxiliary-model, self-speculative decoding for MDMs (LLaDA-8B-Instruct, Dream-7B-Instruct). Confidence-based position selection. Tested on GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval, MBPP. 2.11-3.46x speedup.
collisions.key_difference: SSD uses full-resolution forward pass + hierarchical verification tree (lossless). IGSD uses 16-step coarse draft + confidence threshold partition (approximate). Proposal correctly acknowledges SSD as prior work.
collisions.status_in_proposal: ALREADY ACKNOWLEDGED — proposal revised to cite SSD and reposition IGSD as complementary variant

collisions.paper: Campbell et al., 2025. 'Self-Speculative Masked Diffusions (SSMD).' arXiv:2510.03929. Published 2025-10-04.
collisions.overlap: Self-speculative masked diffusion using attention mask switching. Training-free. ~2x forward pass reduction.
collisions.key_difference: GPT-2 scale, not 8B. Attention mask modification vs. coarse-step draft.

collisions.paper: Han et al., 2026. 'S2D2: Fast Decoding for Diffusion LLMs via Training-Free Self-Speculation.' arXiv:2603.25702. Published 2026-03-26.
collisions.overlap: Training-free self-speculative decoding for block-diffusion LLMs. 4.7x speedup. LLaDA2.1-Mini.
collisions.key_difference: Block-diffusion (partially AR) not fully masked MDM. Different verification mechanism.

collisions.paper: Yang et al., 2026. 'Improving Sampling for Masked Diffusion Models via Information Gain.' arXiv:2602.18176. Published 2026-02-20.
collisions.overlap: Uses 'information gain' as decoding criterion for MDMs — overlapping terminology with original IGSD name.
collisions.key_difference: Does NOT use coarse draft, does NOT partition S_accept/S_refine, does NOT reduce forward passes. IGSD has been renamed to 'Coarse-Step Self-Speculative Denoising' to resolve name confusion.
collisions.status_in_proposal: ADDRESSED — IGSD renamed, name conflict resolved

collisions.paper: Fast-dLLM (Cheong et al., 2025/2026. 'Fast-dLLM: Training-free Acceleration of Diffusion LLM by Enabling KV Cache and Parallel Decoding.' arXiv:2505.22618. Accepted ICLR 2026.)
collisions.overlap: Demonstrates that KV-cache + confidence-aware parallel decoding compose synergistically in MDMs (up to 27.6x combined speedup on LLaDA-8B). This is the closest existing two-method composition study.
collisions.key_difference: Fast-dLLM only studies two methods from the same paper family. No formal orthogonality metric. No cross-family evaluation (no speculative decoding, no AR guidance families). No failure-mode characterization. ComposeAccel's 4-family cross-family study with formal Ortho metric and failure atlas is a distinct and more comprehensive contribution.
collisions.impact: POSITIVE — validates composability research direction; should be cited in intro to motivate extension

collisions.paper: Kolbeinsson et al., 2024. 'Composable Interventions for Language Models.' arXiv:2407.06483.
collisions.overlap: Composability framework for LLM interventions (not inference acceleration). Methodological inspiration for orthogonality metrics.

collisions.paper: Sedykh et al., 2026. 'Not All Denoising Steps Are Equal: Model Scheduling for Faster MDLMs.' arXiv:2604.02340.
collisions.overlap: Step importance analysis for MDLMs; single-method, no composition.

candidates.differentiation_notes: 1) IGSD correctly repositioned as analysis vehicle not standalone method. 2) Composability framework remains genuinely novel — no cross-family systematic study exists. 3) Fast-dLLM's two-method synergy observation validates the direction and should be cited. 4) Critical remaining experiment: SSD+M1 composability comparison — both outcomes are publishable but lead to different framing. 5) Training-free focus is clean differentiator from CD4LM/CDLM. 6) Related work must now also cite Fast-dLLM, APD (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight, arXiv:2506.00413), CD4LM (arXiv:2601.02236), CDLM (arXiv:2511.19269).
critical_remaining_experiments: SSD+M1 composability (4 GPU-hours) — determines if synergy is IGSD-specific or general to self-speculative + KV-cache
IGSD REFINE ablation (2 GPU-hours) — validates frozen-token KV anchor mechanism
tau=0.0 vs. naive uniform T=16 (2 GPU-hours) — resolves acceptance-gate value question
key_paper_additions_to_related_work: Fast-dLLM (arXiv:2505.22618) — cite as closest prior two-method composition study
APD (arXiv:2506.00413, NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight) — new AR-guided MDM acceleration method
CD4LM (arXiv:2601.02236) — training-based consistency distillation for LLaDA-8B
CDLM (arXiv:2511.19269, MLSys 2026) — training-based consistency MDM acceleration

candidates.title: Consistency Distillation for Masked Diffusion Language Models
candidates.score_change_from_prior_report: -2 (from 6/10 to 4/10 due to CD4LM and CDLM)

collisions.paper: CD4LM. arXiv:2601.02236. 'CD4LM: Consistency Distillation and aDaptive Decoding for Diffusion Language Models.' January 5, 2026.
collisions.overlap: Consistency distillation (DSCD: Discrete-Space Consistency Distillation) applied to LLaDA-8B-Instruct. Evaluated on GSM8K (5.18x speedup) and HumanEval coding (3.30x speedup with +2.2% accuracy). Strict Pareto dominance over baseline. Uses CAD (Confidence-Adaptive Decoding) paired with DSCD.
collisions.key_difference: CD4LM fine-tunes the full LLaDA-8B model (not a lightweight adapter on frozen weights). Cand_b's proposal uses ~50M param adapter on frozen LLaDA-8B. If cand_b can demonstrate parameter-efficient frozen-backbone consistency distillation matching CD4LM's performance, there is a novelty claim. However, the performance bar is set by CD4LM.
collisions.severity_note: HIGH — directly implements consistency distillation for LLaDA-8B on reasoning/coding benchmarks

collisions.paper: CDLM. arXiv:2511.19269. 'CDLM: Consistency Diffusion Language Models for Faster Sampling.' November 2025. Accepted: MLSys 2026.
collisions.overlap: Consistency modeling + block-wise causal attention for LLaDA and Dream. 3.6x-14.5x lower latency. Evaluated on math and coding. Accepted at top systems venue (MLSys 2026). GitHub code available.
collisions.key_difference: CDLM requires architectural modification (block-wise causal attention) and full fine-tuning, not a lightweight adapter on frozen weights. But CDLM's MLSys 2026 acceptance means the consistency-for-DLM space is now published at a premier venue.
collisions.severity_note: HIGH — accepted at MLSys 2026, demonstrates feasibility and sets performance bar

collisions.paper: T3D (Zhang et al., 2026. arXiv:2602.12262). 'T3D: Few-Step Diffusion Language Models via Trajectory Self-Distillation.'
collisions.overlap: Trajectory self-distillation for DLLMs. Reduces step count.
collisions.key_difference: Applied to smaller models, not LLaDA-8B-Instruct. Does not achieve 1-4 step inference on reasoning/coding. Different objective (DDO).

collisions.overlap: Discrete flow-matching consistency training. 128x faster sampling. Perplexity benchmarks only.
collisions.key_difference: Different model family (discrete flow matching, not masked absorbing diffusion). Perplexity benchmarks only, not reasoning/coding.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Cand_b's only remaining differentiation is the frozen-backbone lightweight adapter approach (~50M params). CD4LM and CDLM both require full fine-tuning or architectural modification. IF cand_b can demonstrate: (1) frozen-backbone consistency distillation works at LLaDA-8B scale with comparable speedup, AND (2) parameter efficiency / no catastrophic forgetting provides a clear deployment advantage, THEN cand_b has a narrowly defensible novelty claim. However, the window is closing rapidly. Pivot to cand_b only if cand_a completely fails AND there is strong empirical differentiation from CD4LM/CDLM.
candidates.pivot_condition: cand_a full-scale Ortho < 0.7 AND SSD shows no differentiated composability — AND frozen-backbone adapter approach shows strong differentiation from CD4LM/CDLM

candidates.title: Batched MDM Inference: Roofline Analysis and Convergence-Stratified Scheduling

collisions.paper: Gao et al., 2025. 'Self Speculative Decoding for Diffusion Large Language Models (SSD).' arXiv:2510.04147 (Figure 1).
collisions.overlap: SSD paper shows TPS vs. batch size for LLaDA-8B-Instruct demonstrating memory-bound behavior at batch size <= 8. Single figure observation.
collisions.key_difference: Single figure, not a systematic roofline analysis. No convergence-stratified batching scheduler. No arithmetic intensity characterization.

collisions.paper: 'How Efficient Are Diffusion Language Models? A Critical Examination of Efficiency Evaluation Practices.' arXiv:2510.18480.
collisions.overlap: Meta-analysis of DLM efficiency evaluation practices. Discusses compute-bound vs. memory-bound behavior across batch sizes.
collisions.key_difference: Evaluation methodology critique, not a roofline analysis or scheduling optimization. No convergence-stratified batching.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No systematic roofline characterization of MDM batched inference exists. The paper must go substantially beyond the SSD Figure 1 observation and the efficiency evaluation critique. Cand_c is now the highest-novelty backup candidate given cand_b's deterioration. Measurement-only version (roofline analysis + compute vs. memory bound characterization) is publishable at a workshop. Full version (convergence-stratified batching + KV-budget-aware scheduler) targets EMNLP/AAAI.
candidates.pivot_condition: cand_a full-scale Ortho < 0.7 — preferred over cand_b given current novelty landscape

overall_notes: The April 14 update confirms cand_a's composability framework remains genuinely novel after successful proposal revision. The paper is viable for NeurIPS 2026 with conditions. Cand_b has significantly deteriorated as a backup option due to CD4LM and CDLM. Cand_c is now the preferred backup. The pivot priority is: cand_a (primary) → cand_c (backup) → cand_b (last resort, only if frozen-backbone approach provides strong differentiation).
related_work_required_additions: Fast-dLLM (arXiv:2505.22618, ICLR 2026) — KV-cache + parallel decoding synergy in MDMs
APD: Adaptive Parallel Decoding (arXiv:2506.00413, NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight) — AR-guided MDM acceleration
CD4LM (arXiv:2601.02236) — training-based consistency distillation for LLaDA-8B
CDLM (arXiv:2511.19269, MLSys 2026) — consistency modeling for DLM acceleration
Info-Gain Sampler (arXiv:2602.18176) — information gain decoding for MDMs
